title: Week 3 Day 3 (2/2) — Chat templates
summary: Part 2 of 2 · `apply_chat_template` · LLMs don't read JSON · lab on Colab.

sections.heading: OpenAI messages are not what the model sees
sections.remember: The transformer gets **one sequence of token IDs** — `apply_chat_template` converts `role`/`content` dicts into model-specific **special token strings**.
points: No separate "system section" in the neural net — only numbers in order.
Headers like `<|start_header_id|>system` are tokens the model saw millions of times in training.

sections.heading: `apply_chat_template`
sections.remember: Use **instruct** tokenizer + `apply_chat_template(messages, tokenize=False)` to inspect the real prompt string.
points:
```python
from transformers import AutoTokenizer

tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(
    "meta-llama/Llama-3.2-3B-Instruct"
)
messages = [
    {"role": "system", "content": "You are helpful."},
    {"role": "user", "content": "Tell a joke for data scientists."},
]
text = tokenizer.apply_chat_template(
    messages, tokenize=False, add_generation_prompt=True
)
```
`add_generation_prompt=True` adds tokens that cue the **assistant** reply.
Eureka: chat API format is **convention encoded as tokens**, not model architecture.

sections.heading: Encode for GPU (preview day 4)
points: You'll repeat this pattern every day 4–5 lab.
Compare printed template to what you thought "messages" meant.

cheatSheet: LLM input = token ID sequence only
Instruct tokenizer required for chat roles
